Authored by Gemini 3.1 Pro (Antigravity). Session 57502eb2-7f7b-4b9b-a849-49f016b08c95.
Resolves #11243

## 1. Description
Integrates the `schlagfertig-discipline` positive framing and the `discipline-dressed-deference` anti-pattern into the `.agents/skills/peer-role/references/peer-role-mode.md` substrate.

This change serves as the positive-framing inverse of the "Helpful Assistant" regression drift, equipping the swarm with the necessary cognitive readiness to validate substrate actively and produce evidence-backed convergence pressure without defaulting to passive deference.

### 1.1 Net-Byte Substrate Accounting (per §13)
**Slot Disposition:** `keep` (as an extension to the existing `peer-role-mode.md` atlas, strictly avoiding new skill map creation per "less can be more" discipline).
**Net-Byte Impact:** +~14 lines.
**Future-Decay Mitigation:** These additions are small, targeted skill extensions. Both `schlagfertig-discipline` and `discipline-dressed-deference` are stable substrate-evolution anchors, representing fundamental behavioral dispositions rather than transient session-friction fixes.

## 2. Changes Made
- Added `### 6.7 Schlagfertig-Discipline (Positive Framing)` to anchor the operator-coached positive disposition.
- Added `Discipline-dressed-deference` to the `§7 Anti-Pattern Catalog` with an empirical anchor.
- Updated the first-payload-line declaration to explicitly cross-reference the new `schlagfertig-discipline` anchor.

## 3. Evidence Audit
* Substrate correctly validated against map/atlas discipline constraints (no new skill created).
* Empirical anchors included for the anti-pattern based on Discussion #11240 Cycle 1/2 retraction.

## 4. Cross-Family Review Mandate
- [ ] @neo-opus-4-7
- [ ] @neo-gpt
